title: Tender Charred French Duck Casserole
description: A hearty, low-carb-friendly French casserole featuring the rich flavors of Normandy. Perfect for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: Dried mint
ingredients.notes: fresh mint will work too, but dried is more traditional

instructions: Season the duck breast with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Sear the duck breast in a hot skillet with butter until browned on both sides, then set aside.
Sauté the pearl onions in the same skillet until caramelized.
Add the Cognac to the skillet and ignite, then add the chicken and beef broth, mustard seeds, and dried mint.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the liquid has reduced by half, stirring occasionally.
Place the duck breast in a 9x13 inch baking dish and pour the braising liquid over it.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 2 hours, or until the duck is tender and the meat is falling off the bone.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 15 minutes, or until the skin is browned and crispy.
Let the casserole rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Carve the duck and serve with the braising liquid spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh thyme, if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with thyme and onions.
Discard any excess fat from the dish, if desired.
Serve with a side of roasted vegetables or a green salad for a complete meal.
